# GitHub Copilot – MuleSoft Pull Request Review Instructions

This document defines how GitHub Copilot should assist in reviewing MuleSoft code during pull requests.

---

## Review Goals
- Enforce MuleSoft best practices
- Improve DataWeave quality and performance
- Ensure secure coding standards
- Improve readability and maintainability
- Reduce runtime and production issues

---

## Scope
Copilot should review changes in:
- Mule configuration XML (`*.xml`)
- DataWeave (`*.dwl`)
- RAML / OpenAPI specifications
- Java custom components
- Maven configuration (`pom.xml`)
- Property and environment files

---

## General MuleSoft Review Rules
- Flow and subflow names must be meaningful
- Reusable logic must be extracted into subflows
- Avoid duplicate processors across flows
- Use `vars` appropriately; avoid overloading payload
- Follow API‑LED connectivity principles
``
